arts.ttl: Newcastle vs Arsenal – Combined XI ahead of semi-final clash
arts.sum: Newcastle United host Arsenal in the second leg of their Carabao Cup semi-final this evening. The Magpies are on the verge of reaching Wembley after winning 2-0 in the first meeting at the Emirates. However, Eddie Howe’s side will not be complacent with Arsenal arriving in the North East in good form. arts.ttl: Arsenal have two new signings and nobody has noticed after Mikel Arteta transfer masterclass
arts.sum: Two years on from handing Ethan Nwaneri his Premier League debut at the age of 15, the crown jewel of Arsenal's academy had played limited minutes. But Lewis-Skelly has become even more important to Arsenal and Arteta than Nwaneri of late.

arts.ttl: Stunning new St James' images revealed as Newcastle United plans imagined by AI
arts.sum: Excitement is growing over plans for Newcastle United's St James' Park redevelopment, with talk growing of a new £1.2bn stadium. A state of the art glass surrounding would give fans an eye-catching walk up to the stadium with brand new floodlights and stands that level the ground to a consistent height across all sides.

arts.ttl: Newcastle United poised to agree striker deal after deadline day
arts.sum: Newcastle United are reportedly set to agree to the departure of young striker Garang Kuol in a deal that could go through even after the transfer deadline. arts.ttl: “I want to stay” – Arsenal man makes future hopes clear, summer exit in his sights
arts.sum: Arsenal midfielder Albert Sambi Lokonga has admitted he wants to make his move to Sevilla a permanent one this summer. Mucho Deporte cover comments from the Belgian today in which he admits he’s like to continue at the Spanish club next season after joining on loan from Arsenal this season. arts.ttl: 'Unstoppable' £48m winger who Mikel Arteta 'would love to sign' says he's better than Lamine Yamal
Newcastle United drop triple early team news hint before Arsenal amid nervy Joelinton wait
arts.sum: Eddie Howe's team are looking to return to the home of English football for the second time in three years on a search for the club's first major trophy in 70 years. Howe, on the other hand, enters having fallen to defeat against Fulham.
